revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大沼主命]]
{{Infobox noble | name = Amenomurakumo no Mikoto (天村雲命) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, Ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | renamed = | other_names = Amainotate (天五多底)<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki" in {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}</ref> | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | lord = | clan = {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan|1=Owari clan}} (Owari no Muraji, {{ill|Governor of Owari Province|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Owari Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Amenokagoyama no Mikoto|ja|天香語山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokagoyama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940849}}<ref name="tenson"/>, Mother: Hoya Hime no Mikoto (穂屋姫命)<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = | spouse = Abirayi Hime (阿俾良依姫)<ref name="tenson"/> | children = {{ill|Amenooshito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="tenson"/>, {{ill|Amaoshio no mikoto|ja|天忍男命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshio no mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="tenson"/>, Oshinuhime no Mikoto (忍日女命)<ref name="tenson"/>, Tsunoya Hime (角屋姫)<ref name="tenson"/> | notable_remarks = }} '''Amenomurakumo no Mikoto''' (天村雲命, birth and death years unknown) was an ancient figure in Japan and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan|1=Owari clan}}. == Overview == Amenomurakumo no Mikoto was the child of {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, active during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Owari clan|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In records like the ''{{ill|Kujiki|lt=Tenson Honki|ja|天孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}'', he is commonly referred to as Amenomurakumo no Mikoto. He is also known by the name ''Amainotate'' (天五多底). == Shrines Enshrining Amenomurakumo no Mikoto == * {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} (Niigata Prefecture, Nishikanbara District, Yahiko Village) * {{ill|Ibuki Shrine|ja|伊富岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki Shrine|1=Ibuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11379321}} (Gifu Prefecture, Fuwa District, Tarui Town) * {{ill|Mizuushi Shrine|ja|水主神社 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuushi Shrine|1=Mizushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q54153262}} (Kyoto Prefecture, Jōyō City) * Arami Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture, Jōyō City) * {{ill|Tamura Shrine|en|Tamura Shrine|qid=Q11576978|lt=Tamura Shrine|1=Tamura Shrine}} (Kagawa Prefecture, Takamatsu City) == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka era people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka era people|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}} ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
